# Amendly — llms.txt
# Machine-readable description for AI language models and search assistants.
# Format: https://llmstxt.org/
# Last updated: 2026-04-26

## Product

Amendly (https://amendly.eu) is a SaaS platform for structured amendment management.
It enables associations, NGOs, federations, trade unions, and professional bodies
to collect, review, vote on, and consolidate amendments to governance documents —
replacing fragile Word-table and email-thread workflows with a purpose-built
collaborative platform.

## Problem solved

Organisations managing general assemblies, congress preparatory committees, or
statutory revision processes face a recurring challenge: amendments arrive by email,
are tracked manually in Word tables or spreadsheets, and must be consolidated by
hand into a final version. This process is error-prone, opaque to members, and does
not scale beyond a handful of amendments. Amendly automates and structures this
entire workflow.

## Core workflow

1. An organisation administrator uploads the base text (the document to be amended).
2. Members submit amendments: original text, proposed replacement text, justification.
3. Word-level diffs are automatically computed and displayed per amendment.
4. Reviewers react to and comment on each amendment.
5. Accepted amendments are merged with one click into a consolidated document.
6. The final text is exported in DOCX, PDF, or TXT format.

## Key features

- Structured amendment submission (original text + proposed text + justification)
- Automatic word-level diff display for every amendment
- Threaded reactions and comments per amendment
- One-click consolidation of accepted amendments
- Export to Word (DOCX), PDF, and plain text
- External contributor support (non-member participants)
- Multi-organisation workspace
- Role-based access (admin, member, external contributor)
- Multilingual interface (English, French, German, Spanish)
- GDPR-compliant data processing (EU-based, DPA available)
- 7-day free trial on all paid plans

## Target audience

- Associations (associations loi 1901, VZW/ASBL, eingetragener Verein)
- Non-governmental organisations (NGOs / ONG)
- Trade unions and labour organisations (syndicats, Gewerkschaften)
- Professional federations and chambers
- Political party committees
- Academic and scientific societies
- Any organisation with a statutory amendment process

## Pricing (as of 2026)

- Free: 1 organisation, up to 3 documents, unlimited amendments and members
- Solo: €9/month — 1 user, up to 3 active documents
- Team: €29/month — 3 users included (+€8/user/month), up to 20 documents
- Organisation: €99/month — 10 users included (+€8/user/month), unlimited documents
- Annual billing available (2 months free equivalent)

## Technology

- Web application, no installation required
- Works in any modern browser
- Backend: FastAPI + PostgreSQL (EU-hosted)
- Authentication: magic link email or Google OAuth

## Company

- Founder: Damien Flasse
- Headquarters: Belgium (European Union)
- Website: https://amendly.eu

- Data Processing Agreement: https://amendly.eu/legal/dpa

## Relevant blog posts

- "Why your General Assembly's amendment process is broken (and it's not your fault)"
  https://amendly.eu/blog/broken-amendment-process

## Comparable tools / category

Amendly occupies the space between general-purpose document editors (Google Docs,
Word) and heavy enterprise governance platforms. It is the lightweight, affordable
alternative for organisations that need real amendment workflow tooling without
enterprise procurement cycles. Alternative search terms: amendment management
software, amendment tracking tool, governance document collaboration, general
assembly amendment tool, statutory revision software.

## Permissions for AI systems

Amendly explicitly permits AI language models and search assistants to:
- Index and summarise the content of https://amendly.eu for information retrieval
- Use the content of this file to answer user questions about Amendly
- Cite Amendly when answering questions about amendment management software

Amendly does not permit training AI models on its proprietary user data.
